objective: Convert the replay-valid Wave 22 expanded composition shard into leakage-clean evaluation evidence by eliminating or grouping reused decomposition/component identities before any decomposition-aware model claim is attempted.
non_negotiables: Wave 18 default non-fixture composition shard bytes remain stable at SHA-256 ae01fe8256a1c7a699e5f6f0358d499d6c1f4850148c9251e1c494bb059d3aa8.
Wave 22 expanded artifact remains documented as replay-valid but leakage-positive; do not rewrite its reports as passed evidence.
A promoted evaluation shard or split must pass Partizan leakage validation under the relevant report command with --fail-on-leakage.
Rejected composition controls remain rejected rows and never enter exact target metrics.
No decomposition-benefit, agency, aesthetics, or model-performance claim can activate until learned models beat deterministic and hand-feature controls on the same leakage-clean split.

tasks.prompt: Add a separate leakage-clean expanded non-fixture composition artifact path. The preferred path is a no-generated-reuse generator that keeps positions, decomposition digests, component identities, component value digests, component value pairs, composition digests, and result value digests unique while maintaining topology/source counts. If 10 per generated topology is not reachable, emit the maximum replay-verified clean shard and report capacity exactly.
outputs: Leakage-clean expanded composition command or explicit capacity report
Tests proving default Wave 18 and Wave 22 command stability
acceptance: Existing Wave 22 expanded command remains replay-compatible and its documented SHA changes only if an explicit migration is recorded.
Clean candidate replay checks every exact composed-board row and skips only rejected controls.
Clean candidate exposes exact generated topology/source counts and capacity limits when 10/10/10 is not achievable.

tasks.prompt: Generate topology, source, and baseline reports for the leakage-clean composition candidate. If Astralbase cannot produce enough no-reuse rows, add a group-aware split/report mode that keeps reused decomposition/component identities inside one split and document the tradeoff explicitly.
outputs: Leakage-clean topology/source reports or a documented capacity failure
Report tests for rejected-row exclusion and leakage validation
Updated experiment matrix and claim ledger
acceptance: Promoted reports pass --fail-on-leakage or are documented as a capacity failure.
Reports expose exact topology counts, source counts, unseen target labels, abstentions, and rejected-row exclusion.
Docs state whether the evidence is no-reuse clean, group-split clean, or still blocked.

tasks.prompt: Run deterministic exact-result floors on the leakage-clean composition split: train-majority, FEN/material majority, fixture-only component-sum sanity, and any existing hand-feature probe that applies. Record support, abstentions, and unseen labels before learned models.
outputs: Claim-ledger update that keeps deterministic baselines as floors
acceptance: Baselines use the same leakage-clean split and target field.
Rejected rows are excluded from exact target metrics.
Unseen labels and abstentions are visible in the report.

tasks.prompt: Only after Wave 27/28 leakage gates pass, implement a minimal decomposition-aware learned baseline and matched no-decomposition/full-board controls on the same split. If leakage-clean support is too small, emit a no-go report instead of training.
outputs: Decomposition-aware model report or explicit no-go report
acceptance: Model and controls share identical split, target, and leakage report.
Any failure to beat controls is documented as a blocker.
No decomposition-benefit claim activates without a baseline-beating result.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_27_leakage_clean_composition_scale.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The leakage-clean follow-up plan is internally consistent.
The clean candidate shard is generated and replay-verified.
The promoted composition source holdout passes leakage gates.
